channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Greetings from London, where Banksy's flag man is a warning cry
item.description: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.
item.content:encoded: As he marches on, his flag blows back into his face, leaving him unaware he is only a step away from a perilous fall. As usual with Banksy's art, the statue presents a sharp critique of society.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Ni

item.title: Longtime public servant 'guardedly patriotic' as America turns 250
item.description: At age 72, Houston-area county commissioner Rodney Ellis has seen a lot of both progress and setbacks for America. He's celebrating America's birthday but has concerns about her future.
item.content:encoded: At age 72, Houston-area county commissioner Rodney Ellis has seen a lot of both progress and setbacks for America. He's celebrating America's birthday but has concerns about her future.
item.dc:creator: John Burnett

item.title: How does the Kennedy Center board make decisions? This legal filing sheds some light
item.description: Last year, Trump dismissed Biden-appointed Kennedy Center board members and replaced them with loyalists. A new filing by the Center's lawyers reveals how its board of trustees makes decisions.
item.content:encoded:
Last year, Trump dismissed Biden-appointed Kennedy Center board members and replaced them with loyalists. A new filing by the Center's lawyers reveals how its board of trustees makes decisions.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair

item.title: Meta considered buying Kalshi before developing its own prediction market app
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg met with Kalshi's CEO last year about a potential deal, but talks did not move forward. Now Meta is making its own prediction market app.
item.content:encoded:
Mark Zuckerberg met with Kalshi's CEO last year about a potential deal, but talks did not move forward. Now Meta is making its own prediction market app.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon/AFP and Aaron Schwartz/Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: 'Dead but Dreaming of Electric Sheep' is full of beautifully written grotesqueries
item.description: Paul Tremblay has made a career of pushing the horror genre in new directions. This time, a woman must use a video-game-like controller to get a man, stuck in a vegetative state, across the country.
item.content:encoded: Paul Tremblay has made a career of pushing the horror genre in new directions. This time, a woman must use a video-game-like controller to get a man, stuck in a vegetative state, across the country.
item.dc:creator: Gabino Iglesias

item.title: 'The Perfect Moment' makes the case that culture wars have 'completely eaten America'
item.description: Author Isaac Butler explains how the religious right embraced culture wars, starting in the 1970s with school book bans, and continuing now with the Trump administration's efforts to defund the NEA.
item.content:encoded: Author Isaac Butler explains how the religious right embraced culture wars, starting in the 1970s with school book bans, and continuing now with the Trump administration's efforts to defund the NEA.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Tricks to help you spend less money at restaurants
item.description: Personal finance and nutrition experts share simple strategies that make it possible to eat out without spending a fortune. One tip? You might have to let go of your fast food delivery habit.
item.content:encoded: Personal finance and nutrition experts share simple strategies that make it possible to eat out without spending a fortune. One tip? You might have to let go of your fast food delivery habit.
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: How a stranger helped a man falling on the subway without damaging his dignity
item.description: When Lia Eastep was 21, her father had a stroke which left him unsteady on his feet. His physical limitations almost caused an accident on a subway, but a stranger stepped in.
item.content:encoded: When Lia Eastep was 21, her father had a stroke which left him unsteady on his feet. His physical limitations almost caused an accident on a subway, but a stranger stepped in.

item.title: Here to Help: Jennifer Timmick guides visitors through the stories of American history
item.description: For our series Here to Help, we speak with Jennifer Timmick, volunteer tour guide at the National Museum of American History. She talks about the stories of ordinary people who did extraordinary things.
item.content:encoded: For our series Here to Help, we speak with Jennifer Timmick, volunteer tour guide at the National Museum of American History. She talks about the stories of ordinary people who did extraordinary things.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: The Gambler 500 rally combines trash pickup and old cars
item.description: For the "Gambler 500," people gather on forest service roads in Oregon for a colossal trash pick-up on national forest land, conducted in festive and junky old cars.
item.content:encoded: For the "Gambler 500," people gather on forest service roads in Oregon for a colossal trash pick-up on national forest land, conducted in festive and junky old cars.
item.dc:creator: Matt Martin
